message:
feat(widgets): implement Panel widget with border, title, padding

- Panel wraps child widget with configurable border (square/rounded/double/heavy/ascii)
- Title and subtitle support with left/center/right alignment
- Title truncation for narrow panels (unicode-width correct)
- Padding between border and content
- Border and title color customization
- Scissor clipping for inner content
- 16 comprehensive tests covering all features

Implements bd-3bp.8 requirements (pending epic unblock)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1ho","title":"Rich Core Infrastructure (rich_rust port)","description":"# Rich Core Infrastructure (reference extraction, feature-gated)

## Purpose
This epic tracks optional infrastructure ideas inspired by `rich_rust`.

The plan keeps rich_rust’s best abstractions (Segments / measurement protocol / markup parser), but de-scopes “port everything” into feature-gated extras.

## Reference Source
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/`

## Plan Alignment
From the plan (Chapter 1.4 / 1.5 / Phase 7):
- Segments + measurement protocol are valuable
- export-friendly representations are valuable
- “widget zoo / markdown / syntax / forms” are extras

We treat this epic as Phase 7 **extras infrastructure** unless/until a specific piece is required by the agent harness.

## Tracked Issues (this epic’s children)
- `bd-1ho.2` Console abstraction (optional ergonomic output helpers)
- `bd-1ho.5` Live display system (optional “live updating” view patterns)
- `bd-1ho.6` Filesize formatter (small utility; optional)
- `bd-1ho.7` Logging integration (optional formatting/bridge on top of `tracing`)
- `bd-1ho.8` Extra test suite coverage for the above

Related (rich-derived but implemented elsewhere in the plan tree):
- `bd-1ho.1` Segment system (Cow<str>)
- `bd-1ho.3` TextMeasurement protocol

## Non-Goals
- Locking the kernel behind a large “rich port”
- Introducing new, parallel APIs that duplicate ftui core

## Success Criteria
- [ ] Each child issue clearly states how it integrates with ftui (crate/module + feature flag).
- [ ] Optional infrastructure cannot violate one-writer rule or inline-mode invariants.
- [ ] Tests cover behavior and failure modes; logs are actionable.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:02:34.577032474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T00:23:42.778697422Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:59:07.678702154Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":13,"issue_id":"bd-1ho","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ho.1","title":"Implement Segment system (Cow<str> styled text units)","description":"# Implement Segment system (Cow<str> styled text units)

## Background
Plan Appendix D.1 describes a Segment system inspired by rich_rust.

A Segment is the atomic unit of styled text that can be:
- cheaply borrowed (`Cow<str>`) for string literals / static content
- split at **cell positions** (not byte positions) for correct wrapping

Segments are a bridge:
- higher-level text/layout systems produce Segments
- widgets ultimately paint to `Frame`/`Buffer` using measured/split segments

## Core types
### Segment
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub struct Segment<'a> {
    pub text: Cow<'a, str>,
    pub style: Option<Style>,
    pub control: Option<SmallVec<[ControlCode; 2]>>, // optional; stack-alloc common case
}
```

### Control codes (optional)
Segments may carry control codes for non-textual actions (rare; typically not needed in the kernel).
If we keep them, they must be constrained and documented.

## Key APIs
- `cell_length()` (display width)
- `split_at_cell(cell_pos)` (critical for wrapping with wide chars/ZWJ)
- helpers to split/join lines of segments

## Design constraints
- Splitting MUST respect Unicode scalar boundaries; ideally grapheme boundaries.
- Control segments must not consume width.
- ASCII width fast-path is a later optimization (see bd-v6y) and must not change correctness.

## Tests
- split_at_cell correctness for:
  - ASCII
  - CJK wide chars
  - emoji/ZWJ sequences
- cell_length matches `unicode_width` for the same input
- line splitting preserves styles and does not drop content

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Segment type exists with `Cow<str>` storage.
- [ ] `cell_length` and `split_at_cell` APIs exist and are tested.
- [ ] Segment line-splitting/joining helpers exist.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] Create segment with owned String
- [ ] Create segment with borrowed &str (Cow optimization)
- [ ] Create control segments (newline, home, bell)
- [ ] Calculate cell length accounting for:
  - ASCII characters (1 cell each)
  - Wide characters (2 cells each)
  - Zero-width characters (0 cells)
  - Control segments (0 cells)
- [ ] Split segment at grapheme boundary
- [ ] Strip styles preserving text
- [ ] Join multiple segments
- [ ] Split segments on newlines into Lines
- [ ] Join Lines back into segments

### Zero-Copy Requirements
- [ ] String literals never allocate
- [ ] Short strings use Cow::Borrowed when possible
- [ ] Split returns borrowed segments when feasible
- [ ] Concatenation minimizes copies

### Performance Requirements
- [ ] Cell length calculation: < 50ns for typical segment
- [ ] Split operation: O(1) for ASCII content
- [ ] Join operation: O(n) where n = total chars

### Logging Requirements
- [ ] tracing::trace! for segment creation (debug builds)
- [ ] tracing::warn! for unexpectedly long segments (> 10KB)

### Test Coverage
- [ ] Unit tests for all segment operations
- [ ] Property test: split-join is identity
- [ ] Property test: cell length >= 0
- [ ] Corpus test: Unicode edge cases (ZWJ, RTL, combining)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:02:46.925829242Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T17:50:25.418469745Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T17:50:25.418450880Z","close_reason":"Implemented Segment system with Cow<str> storage, cell_length(), split_at_cell(), SegmentLine, SegmentLines, and line splitting utilities - 40 tests passing","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.1","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:14:50.969799531Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.1","depends_on_id":"bd-10x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:18.847212946Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:43:16.700422525Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-1ho.2","title":"Implement Console abstraction","description":"# Implement Console abstraction (Segment-first, one-writer safe)

## Objective
Provide an optional, ergonomic “console output” helper inspired by rich-style APIs, but adapted to ftui’s constraints:
- **Segment-first** (structured text + style)
- **one-writer safe** (no ad-hoc terminal writes)

This is primarily useful for:
- formatting tool output / logs into wrapped lines
- producing captured output for tests
- generating exportable text streams

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/src/console.rs`

## Scope
- A `Console` type that:
  - accepts `Segment` streams (and/or `Renderable` → `Segment`)
  - performs width-aware wrapping
  - maintains an internal style stack for nested styling
  - can operate in “record/capture” mode for tests

## Output Policy (Critical)
- The Console must not bypass ftui’s output routing.
- Any “print” API must target:
  - an in-memory buffer, OR
  - a `LogSink`/writer abstraction that is already sanitized and one-writer compliant.

## Dependencies / Integration
- Depends on Segment system and markup parsing.
- Should integrate with ftui-style and ftui-text measurement.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for wrapping behavior (including wide/combining chars via existing width utilities).
- Tests for style stack push/pop correctness.
- Capture-mode tests that assert deterministic output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `Console` exists with width-aware wrapping and a style stack.
- [ ] Capture/record mode exists and is used in tests.
- [ ] No API writes directly to stdout/stderr; output routing is explicit.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Style stack: push/pop for nested styling
- [ ] Markup string parsing and rendering
- [ ] Recording mode for test capture

### Color System Requirements
- [ ] Auto-detect color capability from environment
- [ ] NO_COLOR environment variable respected
- [ ] Force color override option
- [ ] Color downgrade for limited terminals

### Output Requirements
- [ ] All output respects current width
- [ ] Long lines wrapped at word boundaries

- [ ] No raw escape codes in output (sanitized)

### Logging Requirements
- [ ] tracing::debug! for width detection
- [ ] tracing::debug! for color system detection

- [ ] Unit tests for color system detection
- [ ] Integration tests with renderables
- [ ] Capture/assertion tests for output verification","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:02:54.445310573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T00:26:24.834611488Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:20.442951992Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:02:54.445310573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:19.913985950Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:26:14.137790103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3mo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:20.973683948Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":12,"issue_id":"bd-1ho.2","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ho.3","title":"Implement TextMeasurement protocol (Appendix D.3)","description":"# Implement TextMeasurement protocol (Appendix D.3)

## Background
Plan Appendix D.3 defines a simple measurement protocol used for layout negotiation.

Key insight:
- measurement is *bounds* (minimum + maximum), not a single width
- bounds compose with simple algebra (union vs stack)

This supports deterministic layout without ad-hoc heuristics.

## Core type (from plan)
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Default)]
pub struct TextMeasurement {
    pub minimum: usize,
    pub maximum: usize,
}

impl TextMeasurement {
    pub const ZERO: Self = Self { minimum: 0, maximum: 0 };

    /// Union: take max of both bounds (for side-by-side layout)
    pub fn union(self, other: Self) -> Self {
        Self { minimum: self.minimum.max(other.minimum), maximum: self.maximum.max(other.maximum) }
    }

    /// Stack: add both bounds (for vertical stacking)
    pub fn stack(self, other: Self) -> Self {
        Self { minimum: self.minimum + other.minimum, maximum: self.maximum + other.maximum }
    }

    pub fn clamp(self, min_width: Option<usize>, max_width: Option<usize>) -> Self {
        let mut result = self;
        if let Some(min_w) = min_width {
            result.minimum = result.minimum.max(min_w);
            result.maximum = result.maximum.max(min_w);
        }
        if let Some(max_w) = max_width {
            result.minimum = result.minimum.min(max_w);
            result.maximum = result.maximum.min(max_w);
        }
        result
    }
}
```

## Integration points
- `ftui-text`: compute measurements for Text/Segments
- `ftui-layout`: use measurements when allocating space for widgets

## Tests
- union/stack/clamp behave exactly as defined
- clamp invariants:
  - minimum <= maximum
  - clamps preserve ordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `TextMeasurement` exists with union/stack/clamp.
- [ ] Unit tests cover composition laws.
- [ ] Adopted by layout/text measurement code paths (call sites tracked in follow-up issues).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:01.763981159Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:44:05.268581429Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:44:05.268517559Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.3","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:20:15.236621599Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:43:15.109855603Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ho.4","title":"Implement border/box drawing character sets (Appendix D.5)","description":"# Implement border/box drawing character sets (Appendix D.5)

## Background
Plan Phase 4 includes: "Border presets and custom".

Appendix D.5 references a border system inspired by charmed_rust and rich_rust:
- reusable border presets (rounded, square, double, heavy, ascii)
- used by Panel/Block widgets
- should degrade gracefully when Unicode box drawing is undesirable

## Scope
- Define a border/box character set type (name it `BorderChars` / `BorderSet` etc; avoid `Box` name collisions).
- Provide presets:
  - Rounded
  - Square
  - Double
  - Heavy
  - ASCII fallback
- Include T-intersections and cross characters for tables where needed.

## Design notes
- Border characters are from Unicode Box Drawing (U+2500..U+257F).
- ASCII fallback uses `+`, `-`, `|`.
- This is a *data* module; actual drawing happens in widgets (`Block`, `Panel`, `Table`).

## Tests
- Presets contain expected characters (sanity checks)
- ASCII fallback is complete and consistent

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Border character set type exists.
- [ ] Presets exist and are documented.
- [ ] Used by Block/Panel widgets (follow-up dependencies tracked).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:07.697787474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:10:49.349187282Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:10:49.349167564Z","close_reason":"Added BorderSet tees/cross + presets (ASCII, Square, Rounded, Double, Heavy) with tests; updated BorderType variants","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.4","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:20:49.805784770Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ho.5","title":"Implement Live display system","description":"# Implement Live display system (optional)

## Objective
Provide an optional “live updating” display helper (progress/status refresh) for cases where we want transient output in a normal terminal stream.

This is **not** the core ftui rendering model (full-buffer diff + presenter). It’s an optional utility inspired by rich-style “live” output.

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/src/live.rs`

## Design Constraints (ftui-specific)
- Must respect the **one-writer rule**: any redirection of output must be explicit.
- Must not compromise inline-mode invariants when used alongside the harness.
- Must have bounded refresh behavior (no busy looping).

## Scope
- A `Live` controller that:
  - renders a `Renderable`/`Segment`-like view repeatedly
  - supports a configurable refresh cadence
  - cleans up cursor/modes on stop/drop
  - provides vertical overflow strategies (crop/ellipsis/visible)

## Integration
- Reuse Presenter encoding for cursor save/restore, clears, etc.
- Reuse Console abstraction for rendering content into segments/lines.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for overflow strategies and refresh cadence logic.
- PTY integration tests for cleanup on stop/drop and on panic.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Live rendering can start/stop cleanly and is idempotent.
- [ ] Refresh cadence is bounded and testable.
- [ ] PTY tests verify cursor/modes are restored after Live stops.
- [ ] Output routing is explicit (no hidden writes that violate one-writer rule).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:18.946085437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T00:26:40.308488926Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.5","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:23.110285746Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:03:18.946085437Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1ho.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:22.582300790Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1ho.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3ky.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:04:23.633608498Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-1ho.5","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: User requested reopening; reverting prior closure.","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:34:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-376","title":"Implement interactive widgets (List, Scrollbar, Input)","description":"PHASE 5 - INTERACTIVE WIDGETS

## Background
Interactive widgets handle user input and state changes. List provides selectable items, Scrollbar visualizes scroll position, Input accepts text entry.

## Technical Requirements

### List Widget
- Items with optional styling per item
- Single/multi selection modes
- Keyboard navigation (up/down/pageup/pagedown/home/end)
- Highlight style for selected item
- Scroll into view on selection change
- Item filtering/search support

### Scrollbar Widget
- Vertical and horizontal orientation
- Track, thumb, and arrow styles
- Thumb size proportional to content ratio
- Click/drag support (if mouse enabled)
- Auto-hide when content fits

### Input Widget
- Single-line text input
- Cursor positioning with blinking
- Text selection (shift+arrow)
- Clipboard operations (copy/paste)
- Placeholder text when empty
- Password mode (masked input)
- Input validation hooks

## Design Rationale
These widgets encapsulate common interaction patterns. State management follows Elm architecture - widgets emit events, parent handles state updates.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List with selection modes
- [ ] List keyboard navigation
- [ ] Scrollbar sizing algorithm
- [ ] Scrollbar orientation support
- [ ] Input cursor management
- [ ] Input text selection
- [ ] All widgets implement StatefulWidget trait
- [ ] Event emission for state changes

## StatefulWidget Trait
pub trait StatefulWidget {
    type State;
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer, state: &mut Self::State);
}

 {"id":"bd-381","title":"Implement syntax highlighting for code blocks","description":"PHASE 7 - EXTRAS: SYNTAX HIGHLIGHTING

## Background
Syntax highlighting is essential for code-centric TUI applications. Feature-gated as optional extra.

## Technical Requirements

### Highlighting Engine
- Use syntect or tree-sitter for parsing
- Support common languages: Rust, Python, TypeScript, JSON, YAML, TOML, Markdown
- Theme integration (VS Code themes, base16, etc.)
- Line number gutter option
- Diff highlighting (additions, deletions)

### API Design

pub struct SyntaxHighlighter {
    syntax_set: SyntaxSet,
    theme_set: ThemeSet,
    current_theme: String,
}

impl SyntaxHighlighter {
    pub fn new() -> Self;
    
    /// Highlight code string

 {"id":"bd-389n","title":"Implement FrameBudgetConfig with sensible defaults","description":"# Implement FrameBudgetConfig with sensible defaults

## Background
Part of Render Budget Enforcement (bd-1iqn). Configuration struct for tuning budget behavior per-application.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Frame budget configuration
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct FrameBudgetConfig {
    /// Total frame budget (default: 16ms for ~60fps feel)
    pub total: Duration,
    
    /// Budget reserved for diff computation
    pub diff_budget: Duration,
    
    /// Budget reserved for ANSI emission
    pub present_budget: Duration,
    
    /// Whether to skip frames when severely over budget
    pub allow_frame_skip: bool,
    
    /// Minimum frames between degradation level increases
    /// Prevents rapid oscillation
    pub degradation_cooldown: u32,
    
    /// Minimum frames at a level before upgrading
    /// Ensures stability before returning to higher fidelity
    pub upgrade_threshold: u32,
    
    /// Whether to log budget overruns
    pub log_overruns: bool,
    
    /// Show visual indicator when degraded (helpful for debugging)
    /// When enabled, shows small icon in corner during degradation
    pub show_degradation_indicator: bool,
    
    /// Callback for degradation events (for telemetry/monitoring)
    pub on_degradation: Option<Box<dyn Fn(DegradationLevel, DegradationLevel) + Send + Sync>>,
    
    /// Cost estimates for common operations
    pub cost_estimates: CostEstimates,
}

/// Estimated costs for operations (for should_degrade checks)
#[derive(Clone, Debug)]
pub struct CostEstimates {
    /// Cost of Unicode box-drawing borders
    pub unicode_border: Duration,
    /// Cost of styled text rendering  
    pub styled_text: Duration,
    /// Cost of animation tick
    pub animation_tick: Duration,
}

impl Default for FrameBudgetConfig {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            total: Duration::from_millis(16),
            diff_budget: Duration::from_millis(2),
            present_budget: Duration::from_millis(4),
            allow_frame_skip: true,
            degradation_cooldown: 3,
            upgrade_threshold: 5,
            log_overruns: true,
            show_degradation_indicator: false,  // Off by default in production
            on_degradation: None,
            cost_estimates: CostEstimates::default(),
        }
    }
}

impl FrameBudgetConfig {
    /// Create config for high-performance mode (8ms budget)
    pub fn high_performance() -> Self {
        Self {
            total: Duration::from_millis(8),
            degradation_cooldown: 2,
            ..Default::default()
        }
    }
    
    /// Create config for low-latency mode (aggressive degradation)
    pub fn low_latency() -> Self {
        Self {
            total: Duration::from_millis(8),
            degradation_cooldown: 1,
            upgrade_threshold: 10,
            ..Default::default()
        }
    }
    
    /// Create config for development (shows indicator, verbose logging)
    pub fn development() -> Self {
        Self {
            show_degradation_indicator: true,
            log_overruns: true,
            ..Default::default()
        }
    }
}
```

## User Experience Features

When `show_degradation_indicator: true`, a small icon appears in the corner:

For monitoring/telemetry, applications can register a callback:
```rust

    metrics::counter!("render.degradation").increment(1);
}));
```

 {"id":"bd-397","title":"Create adversarial tests for escape injection","description":"TESTING - ADVERSARIAL SECURITY TESTS

## Background
FrankenTUI must be secure against untrusted content. Escape sequences in user-provided text could corrupt the terminal or execute malicious sequences. This implements ADR-006 (Sanitize-by-Default).

## Threat Model

### Attack Vectors
1. **Log injection**: Untrusted output contains escape sequences
2. **Title injection**: OSC sequences to change window title
3. **Clipboard injection**: OSC 52 to set clipboard
4. **Cursor corruption**: CSI sequences to move cursor
5. **Mode hijacking**: Enabling raw mode, mouse, etc.

### Example Malicious Inputs
- "\x1b[2J" - Clear screen
- "\x1b[H" - Home cursor
- "\x1b]0;pwned\x07" - Set window title
- "\x1b]52;c;SGVsbG8=\x07" - Set clipboard
- "\x1b[?1049h" - Enter alt screen
- "\x1b[?1000h" - Enable mouse
- "\x1b[31mred\x1b[0m" - Color injection

## Technical Requirements

### Sanitizer Implementation
pub fn sanitize_for_display(input: &str) -> Cow<str> {
    // Fast path: no control characters
    if !input.bytes().any(|b| b < 0x20 || b == 0x7F || b == 0x1B) {
        return Cow::Borrowed(input);
    }
    
    // Slow path: filter control sequences
    let mut output = String::with_capacity(input.len());
    let mut chars = input.chars().peekable();
    
    while let Some(c) = chars.next() {
        match c {
            '\x1b' => skip_escape_sequence(&mut chars),
            c if c.is_control() && c != '\n' && c != '\t' => {
                // Replace with visual representation
                output.push_str(&format!("^{}", (c as u8 + 64) as char));
            }
            _ => output.push(c),
        }
    }
    
    Cow::Owned(output)
}

### Test Categories

#### 1. Escape Sequence Filtering
- [ ] ESC [ ... (CSI) filtered
- [ ] ESC ] ... (OSC) filtered
- [ ] ESC P ... (DCS) filtered
- [ ] ESC _ ... (APC) filtered
- [ ] ESC ^ ... (PM) filtered

#### 2. Control Character Handling
- [ ] NUL (0x00) filtered
- [ ] BEL (0x07) filtered
- [ ] BS (0x08) filtered
- [ ] Tab (0x09) preserved
- [ ] LF (0x0A) preserved
- [ ] CR (0x0D) handled
- [ ] DEL (0x7F) filtered

#### 3. Incomplete Sequence Handling
- [ ] Truncated ESC at end
- [ ] Truncated CSI at end
- [ ] Truncated OSC at end
- [ ] Invalid sequence bytes

#### 4. Raw Passthrough (Explicit Opt-In)
- [ ] raw_text() API exists
- [ ] Loud documentation warning
- [ ] Works for ANSI colors when intended

## PTY Tests

### Test Script
for payload in "${MALICIOUS_PAYLOADS[@]}"; do
    # Run payload through ftui log viewer
    echo "$payload" | ftui-test-harness
    
    # Verify terminal state unchanged
    verify_terminal_state
    
    # Verify no escape sequences in output
    verify_no_escapes
done

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All ESC/CSI/OSC/DCS/APC filtered by default
- [ ] Control characters filtered except tab/newline
- [ ] Fast path for clean input (no allocation)
- [ ] Raw passthrough API documented with warnings
- [ ] PTY tests verify terminal state preserved
- [ ] Fuzz testing for parser robustness

 {"id":"bd-3aqs","title":"Implement output bytes tracking and budget monitoring","description":"# Implement output bytes tracking and budget monitoring

## Background
Plan Section 0.12 defines performance budgets including bytes emitted per present:
- "For small change frames, emitted bytes should be O(changes), not O(screen)"
- "Track and regress-test: bytes/frame for representative harness scenes"

This is critical for ensuring diff-based rendering actually achieves its goal of minimal output.

## Purpose
Track exactly how many bytes are emitted during each present() operation to:
1. Verify O(changes) output size
2. Detect regressions
3. Validate run-grouping effectiveness
4. Profile presenter efficiency

## Implementation

### Counter Integration

```rust
/// Presenter with byte counting
pub struct Presenter<W: Write> {
    writer: CountingWriter<BufWriter<W>>,
    // ... other fields
}

/// Write wrapper that counts bytes
struct CountingWriter<W> {
    inner: W,
    bytes_written: u64,
}

impl<W: Write> Write for CountingWriter<W> {
    fn write(&mut self, buf: &[u8]) -> io::Result<usize> {
        let n = self.inner.write(buf)?;
        self.bytes_written += n as u64;
        Ok(n)
    }
    
    fn flush(&mut self) -> io::Result<()> {
        self.inner.flush()
    }
}

impl<W: Write> CountingWriter<W> {
    fn bytes_written(&self) -> u64 {
        self.bytes_written
    }
    
    fn reset_counter(&mut self) {
        self.bytes_written = 0;
    }
}
```

### Presenter Stats

```rust
/// Statistics from a present() call
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct PresentStats {
    /// Bytes emitted for this frame
    pub bytes_emitted: u64,
    
    /// Number of cells changed
    pub cells_changed: usize,
    
    /// Number of runs (groups of consecutive changes)
    pub run_count: usize,
    
    /// Time spent in present()
    pub duration: std::time::Duration,
}

impl<W: Write> Presenter<W> {
    /// Present with stats collection
    pub fn present_with_stats(
        &mut self,
        buffer: &Buffer,
        diff: &BufferDiff,
        pool: &GraphemePool,
        links: &LinkRegistry,
    ) -> io::Result<PresentStats> {
        self.writer.reset_counter();
        let start = std::time::Instant::now();
        
        let runs = diff.runs(buffer.width());
        let run_count = runs.len();
        
        // ... do actual presentation ...
        
        self.writer.flush()?;
        
        Ok(PresentStats {
            bytes_emitted: self.writer.bytes_written(),
            cells_changed: diff.len(),
            run_count,
            duration: start.elapsed(),
        })
    }
}
```

### Budget Thresholds

```rust
/// Expected bytes per cell change (approximate)
/// Worst case: cursor move + full SGR reset + 3-byte char
const BYTES_PER_CELL_MAX: u64 = 30;

/// Bytes for sync output wrapper
const SYNC_OVERHEAD: u64 = 16;

/// Expected bytes for a frame with N changes
pub fn expected_max_bytes(cells_changed: usize, runs: usize) -> u64 {
    // cursor move per run (10 bytes) + cells * max_per_cell + sync
    (runs as u64 * 10) + (cells_changed as u64 * BYTES_PER_CELL_MAX) + SYNC_OVERHEAD
}

/// Check if output is within budget
pub fn check_budget(stats: &PresentStats) -> bool {
    let budget = expected_max_bytes(stats.cells_changed, stats.run_count);
    stats.bytes_emitted <= budget
}
```

### Logging for Debugging

```rust
impl PresentStats {
    pub fn log(&self) {
        tracing::debug!(
            bytes = self.bytes_emitted,
            cells_changed = self.cells_changed,
            runs = self.run_count,
            duration_us = self.duration.as_micros(),
            bytes_per_cell = self.bytes_per_cell(),
            "Present stats"
        );
    }
    
    pub fn bytes_per_cell(&self) -> f64 {
        if self.cells_changed == 0 {
            0.0
        } else {
            self.bytes_emitted as f64 / self.cells_changed as f64
        }
    }
}
```

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Full Redraw Baseline
```rust
#[test]
fn test_full_redraw_bytes() {
    let buffer = filled_buffer(80, 24); // All cells filled
    let stats = present_full_with_stats(&buffer);
    
    // Full 80x24 = 1920 cells
    // Should be < 60KB (generous budget for full redraw)
    assert!(stats.bytes_emitted < 60_000, 
        "Full redraw too large: {} bytes", stats.bytes_emitted);
}
```

### 2. Small Change (Spinner Tick)
```rust
#[test]
fn test_spinner_update_bytes() {
    // Spinner changes 1 cell
    let diff = BufferDiff::from_changes(vec![(5, 5)]);
    let stats = present_diff_with_stats(&buffer, &diff);
    
    // 1 cell change should be < 50 bytes
    assert!(stats.bytes_emitted < 50,
        "Single cell update too large: {} bytes", stats.bytes_emitted);
}
```

### 3. Status Bar Update
```rust
#[test]
fn test_status_bar_bytes() {
    // 80-column status bar = 80 cells in one run
    let diff = BufferDiff::from_run(0, 23, 80);
    let stats = present_diff_with_stats(&buffer, &diff);
    
    // 80 cells in 1 run should be < 3KB

        "Status bar too large: {} bytes", stats.bytes_emitted);
```

 {"id":"bd-3bp.7","title":"Implement Padding renderable (spacing container)","description":"# Implement Padding renderable (spacing container)

## Objective
Implement a padding container that wraps a child renderable and applies `Sides` padding around it.

This is a basic building block for Panel, layouts, and widget composition.

## Reference (for ideas)
- `legacy_reference_library_code/rich_rust/src/renderables/padding.rs`

## Scope
- A padding wrapper that:
  - computes an inner rect (frame minus padding)
  - renders the child into the inner rect
  - reports measurement bounds that include padding

## Design Notes
- Padding must never produce negative dimensions; clamp at 0.
- If padding consumes all space, child render is a no-op.

## Tests Required
- Unit tests for inner-rect calculation across edge cases:
  - zero padding
  - padding larger than frame
  - asymmetric padding
- Snapshot tests showing padding effect with a simple child.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Padding correctly computes inner rect and clamps at 0.

 {"id":"bd-3bt4","title":"Create ErrorBoundary unit and integration tests","description":"# Create ErrorBoundary unit and integration tests

## Background
Part of Widget Error Boundaries (bd-1euu). Comprehensive tests ensure the error boundary works correctly in all scenarios.

## Unit Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;
    
    // Test widget that panics on demand
    struct PanicWidget {
        should_panic: bool,
    }
    
    impl Widget for PanicWidget {
        fn render(&self, _area: Rect, _buf: &mut Buffer, _budget: &RenderBudget) {
            if self.should_panic {
                panic\!("Intentional test panic");
            }
        }
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn healthy_widget_renders_normally() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: false };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        assert\!(\!boundary.is_failed());
        assert\!(boundary.error().is_none());
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn panic_is_caught() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        // Should NOT panic
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        assert\!(boundary.is_failed());
        assert\!(boundary.error().is_some());
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn fallback_renders_on_error() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 5));
        
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        // Check fallback rendered (red border)
        let content = buf.content_to_string();
        assert\!(content.contains("TestWidget") || content.contains("[\!]"));
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn recovery_works() {
        let mut widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        // First render: panic
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        assert\!(boundary.is_failed());
        
        // Fix the widget
        boundary.inner.should_panic = false;
        
        // Try recovery
        assert\!(boundary.try_recover());
        
        // Render again
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        // Should be healthy now
        assert\!(\!boundary.is_failed());
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn max_recovery_attempts_enforced() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget")
            .with_max_recovery(2);
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        // Initial failure
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        // Try recovery 3 times (should fail after 2)
        assert\!(boundary.try_recover()); // Attempt 1
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        assert\!(boundary.try_recover()); // Attempt 2
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        assert\!(\!boundary.try_recover()); // Should fail - max attempts reached
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn reset_clears_error() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5));
        
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 10, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        assert\!(boundary.is_failed());
        
        boundary.reset();
        assert\!(\!boundary.is_failed());
    }
    
    #[test]
    fn custom_fallback_used() {
        let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
        let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget")
            .with_fallback(|_err| Box::new(Text::raw("CUSTOM FALLBACK")));
        let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 5));
        
        boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 5), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
        
        let content = buf.content_to_string();
        assert\!(content.contains("CUSTOM FALLBACK"));
    }
}
```

## Integration Tests

```rust
#[test]
fn nested_boundaries_work() {
    // Outer boundary
    let inner_widget = ErrorBoundary::new(
        PanicWidget { should_panic: true },
        "InnerWidget"
    );
    let mut outer_boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(inner_widget, "OuterWidget");
    
    let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 10));
    
    // Render - should show inner fallback, not outer
    outer_boundary.render(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 10), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
    
    // Outer should be healthy (inner caught the panic)
    assert\!(\!outer_boundary.is_failed());
}

#[test]
fn terminal_state_preserved_after_panic() {
    // Ensure buffer is in valid state after widget panic
    let widget = PanicWidget { should_panic: true };
    let mut boundary = ErrorBoundary::new(widget, "TestWidget");
    let mut buf = Buffer::empty(Rect::new(0, 0, 20, 10));
    
    // Fill with known pattern
    buf.fill(Cell::new('X'));
    
    boundary.render(Rect::new(5, 5, 10, 3), &mut buf, &RenderBudget::unlimited());
    
    // Area outside boundary should be unchanged
    assert_eq\!(buf.get(0, 0).content, CellContent::from_char('X'));
}
```

 {"id":"bd-3m7u","title":"Headless Rendering Mode for CI Testing","description":"# Headless Rendering Mode for CI Testing

## Background & Motivation

The plan relies heavily on PTY tests for terminal verification (bd-10i.11.2). However, PTY tests are:
- Slow (process spawning overhead)
- Platform-dependent (different PTY behavior)
- Hard to run in some CI environments (Docker, restricted sandboxes)

**The solution:**
- Headless mode renders to in-memory buffer
- No terminal required - works anywhere
- Enables fast snapshot testing in CI
- Complements (not replaces) PTY tests

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Extends bd-10i.11.1 (terminal-model test harness)
- Enhances bd-2u4 (snapshot/golden testing)

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct HeadlessTerm {
    screen: Buffer,
    cursor: (u16, u16),
    captured_output: Vec<u8>,
    style_state: StyleState,
    capabilities: TerminalCapabilities,
}

impl HeadlessTerm {
    pub fn new(width: u16, height: u16) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn process(&mut self, output: &[u8]) { ... }
    pub fn screen_text(&self) -> Vec<String> { ... }
    pub fn assert_matches(&self, expected: &[&str]) { ... }
    pub fn export(&self, path: &Path) -> io::Result<()> { ... }
}
```

## Logging Requirements

All parse errors logged at WARN level with sequence bytes and position. Snapshot mismatches logged at DEBUG with line numbers and diff context.

## E2E Tests

```bash
cargo test --features headless -- --test-threads=1
REGENERATE_GOLDENS=1 cargo test --features headless
./scripts/ci-headless-test.sh --report
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HeadlessTerm type implemented
- [ ] ANSI parsing matches real terminal behavior
- [ ] Snapshot comparison works with clear diff output
- [ ] CI tests run without PTY
- [ ] Integration with existing test framework
- [ ] All ANSI sequences from plan supported
- [ ] Export capability for debugging","notes":"## ANSI Sequences to Support

The headless terminal must support these sequences used by FrankenTUI:

### CSI (Control Sequence Introducer)
- Cursor movement: CUP (H), CUU (A), CUD (B), CUF (C), CUB (D)
- Erase: ED (J), EL (K)
- Scroll: SU, SD
- SGR (Select Graphic Rendition): colors, bold, italic, underline
- Private modes: ?25h/l (cursor visibility), ?1049h/l (alt screen)

### SGR Attributes
- Reset: 0
- Bold: 1, Dim: 2, Italic: 3, Underline: 4
- Foreground: 30-37, 90-97, 38;5;N, 38;2;R;G;B
- Background: 40-47, 100-107, 48;5;N, 48;2;R;G;B

### OSC (Operating System Command)
- OSC 8 (hyperlinks): parse but don't render as link
- OSC 0 (window title): capture but don't use

### Unsupported (should not crash)
- OSC 52 (clipboard)
- DCS/APC sequences
- Unknown CSI sequences

```rust
impl HeadlessTerm {
    fn process_csi(&mut self, params: &[u16], intermediate: &[u8], final_byte: u8) {
        match final_byte {
            b'H' => self.cursor_position(params),
            b'J' => self.erase_display(params),
            b'K' => self.erase_line(params),
            b'm' => self.sgr(params),
            b'A'..=b'D' => self.cursor_move(final_byte, params),
            _ => {
                tracing::warn!(
                    final_byte = final_byte as char,
                    params = ?params,
                    "Unknown CSI sequence"
                );
            }
        }
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-3mo","title":"Implement markup parsing for styled text","description":"PHASE 4/EXTRAS - MARKUP PARSER (Feature-gated)

## Background
Markup parsing allows writing styled text with inline syntax like [bold]text[/bold] or Rich-style markup. Feature-gated as it's not needed by all apps.

## Technical Requirements

### Markup Syntax Options

#### Option A: BBCode-style
[bold]Bold text[/bold]
[italic]Italic[/italic]
[fg=red]Red text[/fg]
[bg=#00ff00]Green background[/bg]
[link=https://example.com]Click here[/link]

#### Option B: Rich-style (Python Rich compatible)
[bold]Bold[/bold]
[red]Red text[/red]
[on blue]Blue background[/on blue]
[link=url]Link text[/link]
[#ff0000]Hex color[/#ff0000]

### Parser Structure

pub struct MarkupParser {
    style_stack: Vec<Style>,
    current_style: Style,
}

impl MarkupParser {
    /// Parse markup string into styled Text
    pub fn parse(&mut self, input: &str) -> Result<Text, MarkupError> {
        let mut spans = Vec::new();
        let mut current_text = String::new();
        
        for token in self.tokenize(input)? {
            match token {
                Token::Text(s) => current_text.push_str(&s),
                Token::StyleStart(style) => {
                    if !current_text.is_empty() {
                        spans.push(Span::styled(
                            std::mem::take(&mut current_text),
                            self.current_style.clone(),
                        ));
                    }
                    self.style_stack.push(self.current_style.clone());
                    self.current_style = self.current_style.merge(style);
                }
                Token::StyleEnd => {
                    if !current_text.is_empty() {
                        spans.push(Span::styled(
                            std::mem::take(&mut current_text),
                            self.current_style.clone(),
                        ));
                    }
                    self.current_style = self.style_stack.pop().unwrap_or_default();
                }
            }
        }
        
        Ok(Text::from_spans(spans))
    }
}

pub enum MarkupError {

    InvalidColor { value: String },

 {"id":"bd-3t87","title":"Progressive Rendering for Large Content with Virtualization","description":"# Progressive Rendering for Large Content with Virtualization

## Background & Motivation

Agent harnesses accumulate massive amounts of data over long sessions:
- Tool outputs can be thousands of lines
- LLM responses stream continuously
- Log files grow unbounded

Naively rendering all content causes O(n) render time, making the UI unusable.

**Why this matters:**
- Agent sessions can run for hours, accumulating 100K+ log lines
- Users expect smooth scrolling regardless of content size
- Memory usage must be bounded
- Render time must be O(visible) not O(total)

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Enhances bd-29v (Viewport/LogViewer) with virtualization
- Works with bd-1iqn (Render Budget) for graceful degradation

## Technical Design

### Core Types

```rust
/// Virtualized content container
pub struct Virtualized<T> {
    /// All items (or reference to external storage)
    items: VirtualizedStorage<T>,
    /// Current scroll offset (in items)
    scroll_offset: usize,
    /// Number of visible items (computed from area height)
    visible_count: usize,
    /// Overscan: extra items rendered above/below visible
    overscan: usize,
    /// Height per item (fixed or measured)
    item_height: ItemHeight,
    /// Scroll velocity for momentum
    scroll_velocity: f32,
}

pub enum VirtualizedStorage<T> {
    /// Owned vec of items
    Owned(Vec<T>),
    /// Reference to external storage with len
    External { len: usize, fetch: Box<dyn Fn(Range<usize>) -> Vec<T>> },
}

pub enum ItemHeight {
    /// All items have fixed height
    Fixed(u16),
    /// Items have variable height, measured lazily
    Variable(HeightCache),
}

/// LRU cache for measured item heights
pub struct HeightCache {
    cache: LruCache<usize, u16>,
    default_height: u16,
}
```

### Virtualized List Widget

```rust
impl<T: RenderItem> Widget for VirtualizedList<T> {
    fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        // Calculate visible range
        let (start, end) = self.visible_range(area.height);
        
        // Apply overscan
        let render_start = start.saturating_sub(self.overscan);
        let render_end = (end + self.overscan).min(self.len());
        
        // Render only visible items
        let mut y = area.y;
        for idx in render_start..render_end {
            if let Some(item) = self.get(idx) {
                let item_height = self.item_height(idx);
                let item_area = Rect::new(area.x, y, area.width, item_height);
                
                if item_area.bottom() > area.y && item_area.top() < area.bottom() {
                    item.render(item_area, buf);
                }
                
                y += item_height;
            }
        }
        
        // Render scrollbar
        if self.len() > self.visible_count {
            self.render_scrollbar(area, buf);
        }
    }
}

impl<T> VirtualizedList<T> {
    fn visible_range(&self, height: u16) -> (usize, usize) {
        let start = self.scroll_offset;
        let visible = match &self.item_height {
            ItemHeight::Fixed(h) => (height / h) as usize,
            ItemHeight::Variable(cache) => {
                // Sum heights until we exceed viewport
                let mut count = 0;
                let mut total_height = 0u16;
                while total_height < height && start + count < self.len() {
                    total_height += cache.get_or_default(start + count);
                    count += 1;
                }
                count
            }
        };
        (start, start + visible)
    }
}
```

### Scroll Handling

```rust
impl<T> VirtualizedList<T> {
    /// Scroll by delta (positive = down)
    pub fn scroll(&mut self, delta: i32) {
        let new_offset = (self.scroll_offset as i32 + delta)
            .max(0)
            .min(self.len().saturating_sub(1) as i32);
        self.scroll_offset = new_offset as usize;
    }
    
    /// Scroll to specific item
    pub fn scroll_to(&mut self, idx: usize) {
        self.scroll_offset = idx.min(self.len().saturating_sub(1));
    }
    
    /// Scroll to bottom (follow mode for logs)
    pub fn scroll_to_bottom(&mut self) {
        if self.len() > self.visible_count {
            self.scroll_offset = self.len() - self.visible_count;
        }
    }
    
    /// Enable follow mode (auto-scroll on new items)
    pub fn set_follow(&mut self, follow: bool) {
        self.follow_mode = follow;
    }
    
    /// Called when items are added
    pub fn on_items_added(&mut self, count: usize) {
        if self.follow_mode {
            self.scroll_to_bottom();
        }
    }
}
```

### Momentum Scrolling

```rust
impl<T> VirtualizedList<T> {
    /// Apply scroll velocity (for smooth momentum)
    pub fn tick(&mut self, dt: Duration) {
        if self.scroll_velocity.abs() > 0.1 {
            let delta = (self.scroll_velocity * dt.as_secs_f32()) as i32;
            self.scroll(delta);
            
            // Apply friction
            self.scroll_velocity *= 0.95;
        } else {
            self.scroll_velocity = 0.0;
        }
    }
    
    /// Start momentum scroll
    pub fn fling(&mut self, velocity: f32) {
        self.scroll_velocity = velocity;
    }
}
```

### Memory-Efficient Log Storage

```rust
/// Log storage with bounded memory and optional persistence
pub struct LogRing {
    /// Circular buffer of log lines
    ring: VecDeque<LogLine>,
    /// Maximum lines in memory
    capacity: usize,
    /// Total lines ever received (for accurate scroll positions)
    total_count: usize,
    /// Optional overflow file for persistence
    overflow: Option<OverflowFile>,
}

impl LogRing {
    pub fn push(&mut self, line: LogLine) {
        self.total_count += 1;
        
        if self.ring.len() >= self.capacity {
            if let Some(overflow) = &mut self.overflow {
                // Write evicted line to overflow file
                overflow.write(self.ring.pop_front().unwrap());
            } else {
                self.ring.pop_front();
            }
        }
        
        self.ring.push_back(line);
    }
    
    pub fn get(&self, idx: usize) -> Option<&LogLine> {
        // Map absolute index to ring index
        let ring_start = self.total_count.saturating_sub(self.ring.len());
        if idx >= ring_start && idx < self.total_count {
            self.ring.get(idx - ring_start)
        } else if let Some(overflow) = &self.overflow {
            // Read from overflow (may involve I/O)
            overflow.read(idx)
        } else {
            None
        }
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-3tfu","title":"Implement RenderBudget struct with time tracking","description":"# Implement RenderBudget struct with time tracking

## Background
Part of Render Budget Enforcement (bd-1iqn). The RenderBudget struct tracks elapsed time during frame rendering and manages degradation level.

## Technical Design

```rust
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};

/// Render time budget with graceful degradation
pub struct RenderBudget {
    /// Total time budget for this frame
    total: Duration,
    /// When this frame started rendering
    start: Instant,
    /// Current degradation level
    degradation: DegradationLevel,
    /// Per-phase budgets (optional breakdown)
    phases: PhaseBudgets,
}

/// Budget allocation per render phase
pub struct PhaseBudgets {
    /// Budget for widget tree traversal
    pub traverse: Duration,
    /// Budget for diff computation
    pub diff: Duration,
    /// Budget for ANSI emission
    pub present: Duration,
}

impl RenderBudget {
    /// Create a budget with the given total time
    pub fn new(total: Duration) -> Self {
        Self {
            total,
            start: Instant::now(),
            degradation: DegradationLevel::Full,
            phases: PhaseBudgets::default(),
        }
    }
    
    /// Time remaining in this frame budget
    #[inline]
    pub fn remaining(&self) -> Duration {
        self.total.saturating_sub(self.start.elapsed())
    }
    
    /// Check if we should degrade before an expensive operation
    /// estimated_cost: rough estimate of how long the operation takes
    #[inline]
    pub fn should_degrade(&self, estimated_cost: Duration) -> bool {
        self.remaining() < estimated_cost
    }
    
    /// Advance to next degradation level
    pub fn degrade(&mut self) {
        let old = self.degradation;
        self.degradation = self.degradation.degrade();
        tracing::warn!(
            from = ?old,
            to = ?self.degradation,
            elapsed_ms = self.start.elapsed().as_millis(),
            "Degrading render fidelity"
        );
    }
    
    /// Get current degradation level for widgets to check
    #[inline]
    pub fn degradation(&self) -> DegradationLevel {
        self.degradation
    }
    
    /// Check if budget is exhausted (should skip frame)
    #[inline]
    pub fn exhausted(&self) -> bool {
        self.remaining().is_zero() || self.degradation.skip_frame()
    }
    
    /// Record phase entry for detailed timing
    pub fn enter_phase(&mut self, phase: RenderPhase) {
        // For tracing spans
    }
    
    /// Get elapsed time since frame start
    #[inline]
    pub fn elapsed(&self) -> Duration {
        self.start.elapsed()
    }
}

impl Default for PhaseBudgets {
    fn default() -> Self {
        Self {
            traverse: Duration::from_millis(4),
            diff: Duration::from_millis(2),
            present: Duration::from_millis(4),
        }
    }
}
```

## Why These Defaults
- Total 16ms = ~60fps feel
- 4ms traverse: widget tree can be complex
- 2ms diff: typically fast with good cell comparison
- 4ms present: includes IO overhead
- Remaining ~6ms: buffer for variance

## Test Requirements
- [ ] remaining() decreases as time passes
- [ ] should_degrade() returns true when cost > remaining
- [ ] exhausted() when remaining is zero OR degradation is SkipFrame
- [ ] degrade() logs warning via tracing

- [ ] PhaseBudgets with sensible defaults
- [ ] Time tracking is accurate

## Background & Motivation

Terminal UIs benefit from subtle animations:
- Spinner for loading states
- Fade for transitions
- Pulse for attention

**The solution:**
- Composable animation primitives
- Budget-aware (skip frames under load)
- Zero allocation during animation tick

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-1iqn (Render Budget) for graceful degradation
- Used by widgets like Spinner, Progress

## Technical Design

```rust
pub trait Animation {
    fn tick(&mut self, dt: Duration);
    fn is_complete(&self) -> bool;
    fn value(&self) -> f32;  // 0.0 to 1.0
}

pub struct Fade { progress: f32, duration: Duration }
pub struct Slide { from: i16, to: i16, progress: f32, duration: Duration }
pub struct Pulse { frequency: f32, phase: f32 }

// Easing functions
pub fn ease_in(t: f32) -> f32 { t * t }
pub fn ease_out(t: f32) -> f32 { 1.0 - (1.0 - t) * (1.0 - t) }
pub fn ease_in_out(t: f32) -> f32 { ... }

// Composition
pub fn sequence(a: impl Animation, b: impl Animation) -> impl Animation { ... }
pub fn parallel(a: impl Animation, b: impl Animation) -> impl Animation { ... }
pub fn delay(d: Duration, a: impl Animation) -> impl Animation { ... }
```

Animations must handle edge cases gracefully:

```rust
impl Fade {
    pub fn new(duration: Duration) -> Self {
        // Handle zero duration
        let duration = if duration.is_zero() {
            Duration::from_millis(1)  // Minimum 1ms
        } else {
            duration
        };
        Self { progress: 0.0, duration }
    }
}

impl Animation for Fade {
    fn tick(&mut self, dt: Duration) {
        // Clamp progress to [0, 1]
        let delta = dt.as_secs_f32() / self.duration.as_secs_f32();
        self.progress = (self.progress + delta).clamp(0.0, 1.0);
    }
    
    fn value(&self) -> f32 {
        self.progress  // Already clamped
    }
}

// Easing functions must handle edge cases
pub fn ease_in(t: f32) -> f32 {
    t.clamp(0.0, 1.0).powi(2)  // Clamp input
}
```
